behavior_summary: When the meteor is struck (U8P_METEOR::hit event), the script always plays a meteor impact sound and then destroys the meteor object.

If the U8P_METEOR::hit parameter falls in the range 2..255 (checked as >1 and <0x0100), the script saves that value to a local, generates two small random values, and invokes an effect/notification routine (METHOD::0080) with the randoms and the stored U8P_METEOR::hit value before destruction.
Sound playback is routed/positioned using info derived from the meteor object and specific sound parameter bytes (0x64, 0x1F).
Destruction (Item::destroy) is unconditional and is performed at the end of the handler to remove the meteor from the world.
notable_control_flow: Entry: Event 5 (U8P_METEOR::hit) handler executes on meteor impact.
Unconditional step: run playSFX with sfx id 0x020B and meteor-associated info.

Conditional branch: if [BP+0Ah] > 1 AND [BP+0Ah] < 0x0100 then -> store U8P_METEOR::hit to [BP-02h], call urandom twice to form two parameters, call METHOD::0080 with (first_random+10, second_random, 0x00, stored_hit).
Cleanup: always call Item::destroy([BP+06h]) and return.
open_questions: What exact semantics/payload of METHOD::0080 are (damage application, timed effect, or notification) — only call-site shape is visible, not its implementation.

Whether the stored U8P_METEOR::hit parameter passed to METHOD::0080 is passed by-value or by-address (the disassembly stores it to [BP-02h] and passes that address/value; exact callee expectation is inferred).
Precise meaning of the two immediate sound bytes (0x64, 0x1F) used by playSFX (volume, attenuation, flags) — engine-specific interpretation unknown.
Which in-game actor or system receives METHOD::0080 (target inferred from context but not explicitly resolved here).

main_function.name: U8P_METEOR::hit
main_function.purpose: Handle a meteor being struck: play impact audio, optionally trigger an effect based on the U8P_METEOR::hit parameter and random jitter, then remove the meteor object from the world.
entry_conditions: Invoked as Event 5 (U8P_METEOR::hit) for a meteor object when it is struck.
Parameters: [BP+06h] = meteor item/object reference; [BP+0Ah] = U8P_METEOR::hit value or U8P_METEOR::hit-source identifier.

function_map.name: U8P_METEOR::hit
function_map.role: Process-level handler for meteor impact (plays SFX, conditionally applies effect, destroys item).

function_map.name: U8P_METEOR::playSFX
function_map.role: Play the meteor impact sound (sfx id 0x020B) using meteor-derived info and sound parameters.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide pseudorandom integers used to parameterize METHOD::0080 (two small jitter values).

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Apply or schedule an effect/notification related to the U8P_METEOR::hit using randoms and the stored U8P_METEOR::hit value (inferred).

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Remove/destroy the meteor object instance from the world.

external_calls.purpose: Play meteor impact sound (sfx id 0x020B) with parameters and object-associated routing/positioning info.
Generate two small random values used to create jitter/duration/magnitude parameters for METHOD::0080 (first called with 0x14, second with 0x08).
Invoke an effect application or notification using (first_random+10, second_random, 0x00, stored_hit). Exact effect semantics are inferred (likely damage/effect scheduling or callback).
Destroy the meteor item/object passed in [BP+06h] to remove it from the game.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the meteor is struck (U8P_METEOR::hit event), the script always plays a meteor impact sound and then destroys the meteor object.
